figures.caption: Fig. 1. Overview of DCG-Net. The CDE generates canonical concept-value prototypes (TM ). A vision transformer encodes images into patch-level visual tokens (V ). The DCA module aligns visual and textual representations via bidirectional attention at the concept-value level, generating local evidence and global relevance (αM ), which are gated to produce the initial node features H(0). The PCG models concept-value dependencies using PPMI-initialized edges refined through learnable message passing, resulting in the refined state H(L) that forms the interpretable bottleneck for diagnosis.
TABLE I PERFORMANCE COMPARISON ON PBC AND SKINCON CLASSIFICATION TASKS. BOLD INDICATES THE BEST RESULTS, AND UNDERLINED TEXT DENOTES

Fig. 2. Qualitative explanation of DCG-Net. (A) Input image with predicted diagnosis, concept-value probabilities, and ground-truth labels. (B) Top concept contributions (α×ppresent), showing which clinical findings most strongly support the model’s diagnostic decision. (C) PCG-based relational reasoning, showing each top concept and its associated concept-value nodes, reflecting learned dependencies among concept-value pairs.
